perturbation.original_text: 1.2 (a) 'Confidential Information' shall mean all information received from the other party which the latter has indicated in writing or labelled to be “Confidential”, “Proprietary Information” or with any other comparable legend to similar effect, at the time of disclosure [or if disclosed orally, confirmed in writing by the disclosing party as such within fifteen (15) days after its disclosure], which it may acquire in relation to the other party, including but without any limitation whatsoever:
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 (a) 'Confidential Information' shall mean all information received from the other party which the latter has indicated in writing or labelled to be “Confidential”, “Proprietary Information” or with any other comparable legend to similar effect, at the time of disclosure [or if disclosed orally, confirmed in writing by the disclosing party as such within thirty (30) days after its disclosure], which it may acquire in relation to the other party, including but without any limitation whatsoever:
perturbation.explanation: The change increases the timeframe for confirming orally disclosed information as confidential from fifteen (15) days to thirty (30) days. This creates an in-text contradiction with Clause 2.3, which requires the recipient to give the disclosing party not less than two (2) business days' notice of any disclosure required by law of the other party's Confidential Information. With the changed definition, information disclosed orally, but not yet confirmed in writing within 30 days, isn't technically considered confidential information, so legally required disclosers of the 'confidential information' may not be bound by the 2-day notice, since the clock for defining it as 'confidential' is still running. This introduces ambiguity as to when the 2-day notice rule applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient shall be entitled to make any disclosure required by law of the other party's Confidential Information, but shall give the other party not less than two (2) business days' notice of such disclosure and shall consult with the Discloser prior to such disclosure with a view to avoiding such disclosure if legally possible.
perturbation.justification: The change extends the timeframe for designating orally disclosed information as 'Confidential Information' from 15 to 30 days. This directly conflicts with Clause 2.3, which mandates a two-day notice before legally required disclosure of 'Confidential Information'. During the extended window (16-30 days), information disclosed orally might not yet be officially 'Confidential', creating ambiguity about whether the two-day notice applies, therefore creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall: (a) within one (1) month of completion of the purpose or receipt of a written request from the other party, return to the other party all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the other party's Confidential Information and certify in writing to the
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall: (a) within three (3) months of completion of the purpose or receipt of a written request from the other party, return to the other party all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the other party's Confidential Information and certify in writing to the
perturbation.explanation: The change increases the timeframe for returning confidential information from one (1) month to three (3) months, creating an in-text contradiction with Clause 11, which states that the agreement can be terminated by either party giving the other not less than thirty (30) days’ prior written notice. It is unclear whether documents containing confidential information need to be returned within one month of termination, or three months after.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. TERMINATION This Agreement shall continue to be in force from the date hereof until terminated by mutual consent or by either party giving to the other not less than thirty (30) days’ prior written notice. The provision of Clauses 1 to 8, 11 to 15 and 17 to 21 shall survive any such termination.
perturbation.justification: The change extends the deadline for returning confidential information from one month to three months. Clause 11 allows termination with 30 days' notice. This creates a contradiction: if the agreement is terminated, it's unclear whether confidential information must be returned within one month of completing the purpose (potentially before termination) or within three months after the purpose (potentially after termination), or after termination. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where conflicting deadlines create uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: 4.5 The data will not be transferred or made available to any Third (3rd) Party without the prior written consent of AHPL . The Company shall not use any data provided by AHPL , or any substance that is replicated or derived therefrom for any commercial or profit -generating purpose, or in the conduct of research that is subject to consulting, licensing or other similar legal or co mmercial obligations to another institution, corporation or business entity, unless the provider provides its prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.5 The data will not be transferred or made available to any Third (3rd) Party without the prior verbal consent of AHPL . The Company shall not use any data provided by AHPL , or any substance that is replicated or derived therefrom for any commercial or profit -generating purpose, or in the conduct of research that is subject to consulting, licensing or other similar legal or co mmercial obligations to another institution, corporation or business entity, unless the provider provides its prior written consent.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'prior written consent' to 'prior verbal consent,' we're creating an in-text contradiction with Section 10.1, which says that notices under the agreement must be in writing. If a consent for transferring data is considered a 'notice,' then this change creates a conflict since section 4.5 allows for verbal consent while 10.1 requires written consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1 Except as oth erwise provided in this Agreement, notices which are required to be given under or permitted by this Agreement shall be in writing (unless expressly stated otherwise) and sent to the address of the recipient set out in this Agreement. All notices may be se nt by hand or by AR Registered post or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid and properly addressed to the offices of the parties as specified in this Agreement or to such other address as the party may later specify.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'prior written consent' to 'prior verbal consent' in Section 4.5 directly contradicts Section 10.1, which mandates that all notices under the agreement must be in writing (unless expressly stated otherwise). Since consent can be reasonably interpreted as a type of notice, this creates a clear conflict, making the change an inconsistency that leads to an in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Explanation and the definition for Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction. This is a strong contradiction because it directly breaks compatibility between the two sections, creating a contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the other provisions o f this Agreement, all the terms and conditions of this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective permitted assigns and successors -in-title except that: - (i) neither party shall transfer or assign all or a ny of its rights, obligations or benefits hereunder in whole or in part to any third (3rd) party, without the prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld; (ii) any permitted assignee or transferee shall agre e in writing to comply with all terms and conditions of this Agreement; and (iii) any assignment shall not exceed the existing scope of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the other provisions o f this Agreement, all the terms and conditions of this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective permitted assigns and successors -in-title except that: - (i) neither party shall transfer or assign all or a ny of its rights, obligations or benefits hereunder in whole or in part to any third (3rd) party, without the prior verbal consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld; (ii) any permitted assignee or transferee shall agre e in writing to comply with all terms and conditions of this Agreement; and (iii) any assignment shall not exceed the existing scope of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'prior written consent' to 'prior verbal consent,' we're creating an in-text contradiction with Section 10.1, which says that notices under the agreement must be in writing. If a consent for transferring or assigning rights, obligations, or benefits is considered a 'notice,' then this change creates a conflict since section 12 allows for verbal consent while 10.1 requires written consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1 Except as oth erwise provided in this Agreement, notices which are required to be given under or permitted by this Agreement shall be in writing (unless expressly stated otherwise) and sent to the address of the recipient set out in this Agreement. All notices may be se nt by hand or by AR Registered post or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid and properly addressed to the offices of the parties as specified in this Agreement or to such other address as the party may later specify.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the requirement for consent from 'prior written consent' to 'prior verbal consent'. Section 10.1 of the contract states that notices required under the agreement must be in writing (unless expressly stated otherwise). The explanation correctly points out that if consent for transferring rights is considered a notice, the changed text introduces a direct conflict because section 12 allows verbal consent, while section 10.1 requires written consent. This creates a strong in-text contradiction based on the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition because it provides conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 15.2 Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement, if the dispute is not settled in accordance with Clause 14.1 above, no party shall pr oceed to litigation or any other form of dispute resolution unless the parties have made reasonable efforts to resolve the same through mediation in accordance with the mediation rules of the Singapore Mediation Centre. A party who receives a notice for me diation from the other party shall consent and participate in the mediation process in accordance with this clause. Failure to comply with this clause shall be deemed to be a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 15.2 Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement, if the dispute is not settled in accordance with Clause 15.1 above, no party shall pr oceed to litigation or any other form of dispute resolution unless the parties have made reasonable efforts to resolve the same through mediation in accordance with the mediation rules of the Singapore Mediation Centre. A party who receives a notice for me diation from the other party shall consent and participate in the mediation process in accordance with this clause. Failure to comply with this clause shall be deemed to be a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change incorrectly refers to Clause '14.1' instead of '15.1'. Since, the intention is to follow the steps in Clause 15, this creates an in-text contradiction regarding which dispute resolution process to follow, as one clause directs towards a non-existent clause in the dispute resolution section (Clause 14.1), leading to confusion about the initial steps in resolving disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.1 In the event of any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with or in relation to this Agreement or the existence, validity, termination, application or interpretation of this Agreement or any of its provisions, both parties shall use their best endeavours to settle the dispute informally by agreement between the parties. Both parties shall always act in good faith and co -operate with each other to resolve any disputes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces '14.1' with '15.1' in Clause 15.2. The original text refers to '14.1' which the explanation says directs towards a non-existent clause in the dispute resolution section. The contradicted text is Clause 15.1, which outlines the initial steps for dispute resolution. By incorrectly referencing clause 15.1 which contains the initial steps to resolve a dispute, the changed text introduces an inconsistency regarding the correct procedure to follow as mentioned in the explanation provided. This creates a contradiction because Clause 15.2 no longer correctly refers back to the intended initial step in Clause 15.1, leading to confusion and potential unenforceability, which aligns with the provided definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
